scope_note: QA Langlands Sym2 Reduced Factor cert [407]. Primary sources: Shimura, G. (1975) 'On the holomorphy of certain Dirichlet series' Proceedings of the London Mathematical Society 31 pp.79-98 doi:10.1007/BF01403156 (symmetric square L-functions for GL_2); Gelbart, S. and Jacquet, H. (1978) 'A relation between automorphic representations of GL(2) and GL(3)' Annales scientifiques de l'ENS 11 pp.471-542 doi:10.2307/1971237 (Sym^2 lift GL_2 to GL_3); Kim, H.H. and Shahidi, F. (2002) doi:10.4007/annals.2002.155.837 (functorial product, Sym^3 for GL_2). CLAIM (narrow and falsifiable): For f=2.2.5.1-125.1-a and split prime p≡1(mod5), the product of the two degree-2 non-trivial Sym^2 components at primes above p gives: Sigma_p(Y) = 1-S*Y+(Q+2p^2)*Y^2-p^2*S*Y^3+p^4*Y^4 where S=T^2-2N-4p and Q=(N+2p)^2-2p*T^2. Four checks: (C1) integer coefficients S, Q+2p^2, p^2*S, p^4 in Z (22/22 PASS); (C2) palindrome a3=p^2*a1 and a4=p^4 (22/22 PASS); (C3) Vieta algebraic derivation: c_i=sigma_i(a_p)^2-2p (observer projections) with c1+c2=S and c1*c2=Q verified to 1e-6 (22/22 PASS); (C4) Sym^2 Ramanujan: c_i^2<4p^2 so quadratic factors have complex roots with |root|=p^{-1}, inherited from cert [403] Ramanujan equality sigma_i(a_p)^2<4p (22/22 PASS). The full GL_3/F Sym^2 Euler factor at each prime above p is (1-pY)*(1-c_i*Y+p^2*Y^2); Sigma_p is the product of the quadratic parts. Theorem NT: c_i are observer projections only; all arithmetic uses integer S,Q. Derived 2026-06-13.
